title: Define static validator set mechanism and attestation authorization
markdown:
## Goal

Implement the static validator set mechanism where a list of authorized node public keys is defined as a schema constant, and the `attestationValidator` checks that the attesting node is in the authorized set. This is the simplest trust model (Option A from CONFIRMATION.md section 6).

## Background

Per CONFIRMATION.md section 6 (Trust Model), the simplest approach is a static validator set:

```typescript
export const VALIDATOR_SET = [NODE_A_KEY, NODE_B_KEY, NODE_C_KEY];
export const CONFIRMATION_THRESHOLD = 2;
```

The `attestationValidator` must check: `nodeKey is in VALIDATOR_SET`. The `nodeKey` is extracted from the attestation URI: `immutable://attestation/{envelopeHash}/{nodeKey}`.

Currently, G2-A2-01 implements the attestation validator without this authorization check (it only checks write-once, value=true, and envelope existence). This story adds the authorization layer.

The design also needs to handle the empty-set case: when `VALIDATOR_SET` is empty, any node can attest (open attestation). This allows single-node setups to work without configuration.

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] `VALIDATOR_SET` exported from `libs/firecat-protocol/constants.ts` as `string[]`
- [ ] When `VALIDATOR_SET` is non-empty, `attestationValidator` rejects attestations from keys not in the set
- [ ] When `VALIDATOR_SET` is empty, attestation is open (any key accepted) -- enables single-node mode
- [ ] `rejectionValidator` also checks the validator set (only authorized validators can reject)
- [ ] Tests verify authorization with a populated validator set
- [ ] Tests verify open attestation with an empty validator set
- [ ] Error messages clearly state "Node {key} is not in the authorized validator set"

## Implementation Guide

1. Update `libs/firecat-protocol/constants.ts`:
   ```typescript
   /**
    * Authorized validator public keys.
    * Empty array = open attestation (any node can attest).
    * Non-empty = only listed nodes can attest/reject.
    */
   export const VALIDATOR_SET: string[] = [];
   ```

2. Add authorization check to `attestationValidator` in `libs/firecat-protocol/validators.ts`:
   ```typescript
   // After existing checks...
   if (VALIDATOR_SET.length > 0) {
     const nodeKey = extractSegment(uri, 2); // attestation/{envelopeHash}/{nodeKey}
     if (!nodeKey || !VALIDATOR_SET.includes(nodeKey)) {
       return {
         valid: false,
         error: `Node ${nodeKey} is not in the authorized validator set`,
       };
     }
   }
   ```

3. Add the same check to `rejectionValidator`.

4. Write tests that:
   - Set up a non-empty validator set
   - Verify authorized nodes can attest
   - Verify unauthorized nodes are rejected
   - Verify empty set allows all

## Files to Create/Modify

- `libs/firecat-protocol/constants.ts` -- Add `VALIDATOR_SET` with documentation.
- `libs/firecat-protocol/validators.ts` -- Add authorization checks to attestation and rejection validators.
- `libs/firecat-protocol/validators_test.ts` -- Add authorization tests.

## Testing

```bash
deno test libs/firecat-protocol/validators_test.ts
```

Note: Since `VALIDATOR_SET` is a module-level constant, tests will need to either:
- Use a factory function that accepts the validator set as a parameter, or
- Modify the array in-place for testing (since arrays are mutable references)

The factory approach is cleaner. Consider making validators accept configuration:
```typescript
export function createAttestationValidator(validatorSet: string[]): ValidationFn
```

And exporting the default-configured version:
```typescript
export const attestationValidator = createAttestationValidator(VALIDATOR_SET);
```

## Dependencies

Depends on G2-A2-01 (base validators must exist). Blocks G2-A2-02 (confirmation validator needs to iterate the validator set) and G2-A2-05 (discovery loop needs to know which nodes to expect attestations from).
tasksExcerpt:
- [ ] Wire `pendingValidator` into node receive pipeline (write pending marker after local validation passes)
- [ ] Wire `attestationValidator` — node monitors `immutable://pending/` and writes attestations
- [ ] Wire `rejectionValidator` — node writes rejection with reason string on validation failure
- [ ] Wire `confirmationValidator` — node counts attestations and finalizes when threshold met
- [ ] Implement node discovery loop (poll `immutable://pending/` for new entries)
- [ ] Implement val
